title: ToolCallEnvelope
description: A public tool-validation envelope. Tool metadata is an assertion that the server compares with its trusted, versioned registry; it is never an authorization source.
$comment: Callers cannot register tools or self-assert risk, approval, side effects, identity, policy action, or definition version through this contract.

tool_name.description: Tool identifier asserted by the caller; unknown names fail closed.

input.description: Arguments or output payload validated against the phase-specific trusted schema.

schema.description: Caller assertion of the phase-specific schema; any difference from the registered schema fails closed.

risk_level.description: Caller assertion compared exactly with the registered risk level; it cannot lower server-side risk.

approval_required.description: Caller assertion compared with the registered definition; it does not grant approval.

caller_context.description: Untrusted correlation context. Tenant, subject, approval, evidence, action, risk, and side-effect values are canonicalized, ignored, or checked server-side.

approval_id.description: Identifier for a server-created approval; not sufficient to authorize execution.

approval_execution_token.description: Opaque, single-use grant token accepted only with the exact bound tenant, subject, tool, action, argument hash, and definition version.
